verses.text: Moses and Israel sing, 'I will sing unto the LORD, for he hath triumphed gloriously.' The song. Salvation breaks forth in praise.
Moses and Israel sing, 'I will sing unto the LORD, for he hath triumphed gloriously.' The song. Salvation breaks forth in praise.
Moses and Israel sing, 'I will sing unto the LORD, for he hath triumphed gloriously.' The song. Salvation breaks forth in praise.
Pharaoh's host is cast into the sea, sunk 'as a stone.' Sunk. The enemy's might is nothing before God.
Pharaoh's host is cast into the sea, sunk 'as a stone.' Sunk. The enemy's might is nothing before God.
'Thy right hand, O LORD, is become glorious in power'; the waters stood up at His blast. God's hand. His power both saves and destroys.
'Thy right hand, O LORD, is become glorious in power'; the waters stood up at His blast. God's hand. His power both saves and destroys.
'Thy right hand, O LORD, is become glorious in power'; the waters stood up at His blast. God's hand. His power both saves and destroys.
The enemy boasted to pursue and spoil, but God blew, and the sea covered them. Boast undone. Pride is drowned in a moment.
The enemy boasted to pursue and spoil, but God blew, and the sea covered them. Boast undone. Pride is drowned in a moment.
'Who is like unto thee, O LORD?' He leads forth the people He redeemed. Incomparable. None is like the holy, saving God.
'Who is like unto thee, O LORD?' He leads forth the people He redeemed. Incomparable. None is like the holy, saving God.
'Who is like unto thee, O LORD?' He leads forth the people He redeemed. Incomparable. None is like the holy, saving God.
The nations shall hear and fear; dread falls on Edom and Canaan. Fear spreads. God's act terrifies the peoples ahead.
The nations shall hear and fear; dread falls on Edom and Canaan. Fear spreads. God's act terrifies the peoples ahead.
The nations shall hear and fear; dread falls on Edom and Canaan. Fear spreads. God's act terrifies the peoples ahead.
He will plant them in His sanctuary; 'The LORD shall reign for ever and ever.' He reigns. The song ends in God's eternal kingship.
He will plant them in His sanctuary; 'The LORD shall reign for ever and ever.' He reigns. The song ends in God's eternal kingship.
Miriam and the women answer with timbrels and dancing. Miriam's song. The women join in praise of the Lord.
Miriam and the women answer with timbrels and dancing. Miriam's song. The women join in praise of the Lord.
Miriam and the women answer with timbrels and dancing. Miriam's song. The women join in praise of the Lord.
Three days without water; at Marah the waters are bitter, and the people murmur. Bitter water. Hardly saved, they begin to complain.
Three days without water; at Marah the waters are bitter, and the people murmur. Bitter water. Hardly saved, they begin to complain.
Three days without water; at Marah the waters are bitter, and the people murmur. Bitter water. Hardly saved, they begin to complain.
God shows a tree to sweeten the waters and promises, 'I am the LORD that healeth thee.' Healed. God turns bitterness sweet for the obedient.
God shows a tree to sweeten the waters and promises, 'I am the LORD that healeth thee.' Healed. God turns bitterness sweet for the obedient.
At Elim they find twelve wells and seventy palm trees. Rest. God leads them on to refreshment.
